commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining work is maintainer sequencing, product/security review, and contributor live proof, not a narrow automated repair ClawSweeper can safely make.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive because it introduces a Teams HMAC media WebSocket, a SecretRef shared secret, a larger inbound frame cap, and camera/screen-share forwarding into agent runs without live... commands.review_summary:
Adds the Microsoft Teams voice-call provider stack plus inbound `video.frame` ingestion, recording-gated latest-frame buffering, and a realtime `look_at_screen` tool that sends camera or screen-share frames to embedded agent runs.

PR surface: Source +2458, Tests +1784, Docs +139. Total +4381 across 25 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. Source and tests show the intended new video path, but the external PR still lacks live real-environment proof of a Teams camera or screen-share reaching the agent and producing the spoken result.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining work is maintainer sequencing, product/security review, and contributor live proof, not a narrow automated repair ClawSweeper can safely make.

target.title: feat(voice-call): Microsoft Teams inbound video — let the agent see (camera + screen-share)
target.body:
> **🔗 Stacked on #89724.** This builds on the inbound Microsoft Teams `msteams` voice-call provider from #89724 (not yet merged), so this PR's commit list currently includes #89724's commits too. **Please review only the last 3 commits** — `ingest inbound video frames`, `look_at_screen vision tool`, `recording-gate test`; everything else lands via #89724. **Merge this after #89724.**

## Summary — the agent can *see* the caller

Lets a Microsoft Teams caller's **camera** and **screen-share (VBSS)** reach the agent so it can answer visual questions during the call — *"what's on my screen?"*, *"read this error"*, *"what am I holding?"*. This is the OpenClaw side of inbound vision; the Windows bridge worker (out-of-repo, the same one #89724 uses) already samples and forwards the frames.

## How it works

```
[Teams caller: camera / screen-share]
   → worker samples sparsely → video.frame over the existing HMAC WebSocket   (worker, separate repo)
   → bridge (msteams-media-stream.ts) parses video.frame → onVideoFrame
   → provider buffers the latest frame per source (recording-gated)
   → look_at_screen tool → runs the agent over the frame as an image (vision) → speaks the answer
```

1. **Ingest** (`msteams-media-stream.ts`): new `video.frame` message (`source`, `width`, `height`, `mime`, `dataBase64`) + `onVideoFrame` callback.
2. **Buffer** (`providers/msteams.ts`): latest frame per `camera`/`screenshare`, **recording-gated** (Media Access API — parity with the audio/transcript gate), path-agnostic (streaming + realtime), exposed via `getLatestVideoFrame()`; cleaned up on session end.
3. **See** (`msteams-realtime.ts`): a realtime **`look_at_screen`** tool pulls the latest frame and runs the OpenClaw agent **over the image** — `consultRealtimeVoiceAgent` now forwards an optional `images: ImageContent[]` to `runEmbeddedAgent` (a vision-capable run) — then speaks a concise description.

## Notable details
- **Vision is a side channel:** the realtime speech model is audio-only, so `look_at_screen` runs a separate vision-capable agent over the still frame (prefers screen-share, then camera).
- **Recording gate:** no frame is buffered or processed before Teams recording is active.
- **Inbound WS payload cap raised 64 KB → 2 MB:** JPEG frames dwarf audio control messages; the sender is the HMAC-authenticated worker, so the looser bound only affects the trusted path.
- Exposed only when the agent runtime + a frame source are available; returns a clear *"camera/share not on yet"* message otherwise.

## Tests
- `video.frame` ingestion, recording-gate (frames dropped until recording active), `look_at_screen` no-frame path, and that the latest frame is passed to the agent as an `image`. `tsgo` prod + test clean; voice-call suites green.

## Out of scope (separate follow-ups)
- Outbound calling and the animated avatar (separate branch).
- Meeting dominant-speaker MSI subscription (PR4 worker part) — `look_at_screen` is wired for the **realtime** path; streaming is an easy follow-on.

## Proof
Live 1:1 proof (screen-share → *"what's on my screen?"* → spoken description) to be added after the on-device test; happy to capture whatever format reviewers prefer.

---
*Contributed by the **Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC)**, Dubai 🇦🇪 — building the "Virtual Employee" Teams assistant in production.*
target.author: ahenawy
